alerts.title: Syria ‘not alone’ in locating missing persons, tackling displacement
alerts.summary: Syria is not alone in tackling challenges, including the hundreds of thousands who remain missing during past tumultuous decades, the head of a UN institution said on Thursday&nbsp;as António Guterres departs on his first visit to the country in his current capacity as Secretary-General.

alerts.title: Iran urged to stop protest-related executions
alerts.summary: Independent UN human rights investigators on Thursday called for Iran to immediately halt the execution of 10 men who took part in a protest in the central city of Isfahan earlier this year, warning that some of their relatives have already been called for final visits.

alerts.title: Gaza’s food gains could unravel without sustained aid, UN warns
alerts.summary: Food security and child nutrition have improved across the Gaza Strip largely due to a scale-up in humanitarian assistance and commercial traffic, but progress remains fragile and could quickly reverse without sustained access, funding and recovery support.

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: How to turn preventive diplomacy rhetoric into action
alerts.summary: In the Security Council debate on Thursday on how best to settle disputes between States peacefully, ambassadors provided examples of success and challenges ahead under such themes as effective early warning and how to move from rhetorical support for preventive diplomacy to ensuring more consistent use of its Chapter VI powers under the UN Charter. Follow our live coverage.

alerts.title: Stranded seafarers remain trapped as Hormuz shipping stalls
alerts.summary: As the Iran-US war continues, the key question remains unanswered about how shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will be managed in future – although the UN has reiterated that charging tolls for safe passage runs contrary to international law.

alerts.title: Thousands prevented from returning home to southern Lebanon
alerts.summary: As months of conflict continue to devastate communities across Lebanon, families are being left without shelter, healthcare and other essential services.

alerts.title: World News in Brief: Aid for Ukraine, drone attacks in Sudan, DR Congo deaths, neurological disorders in the Americas
alerts.summary: UN partners launched the&nbsp;Winter Response Plan to help 2.1 million people in Ukraine survive another cold season as the country grapples with the on-going Russian invasion.

alerts.title: From lithium to nickel: How the UN is helping countries make the most of critical minerals
alerts.summary: The world's shift to clean energy is driving huge demand for critical minerals such as lithium, nickel, cobalt and copper.&nbsp;

alerts.title: Israeli attacks on Gaza intensify amid surge of West Bank settler violence
alerts.summary: Amid intensified deadly Israeli attacks in Gaza, the UN chief and top officials condemned the killings while a surge violence rocked the West Bank and aid agencies scrambled to help Palestinians.

alerts.title: Guterres calls for stepping up diplomatic efforts to end US-Iran conflict
alerts.summary: The United Nations again called for restraint as war ramps up in the Middle East, underscoring the need for dialogue and an end to attacks on “vital infrastructure” across the region.

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: Spotlight on Syria as Guterres heads to Damascus
alerts.summary: The Security Council discussed the situation on the ground in Syria amid reports of an Israeli attack in occupied territory and of a multi-national oil pipeline to counter the closure of the critical Strait of Hormuz – still the focus of the ongoing US-Iran war. The meeting follows UN independent investigators’ findings that security conditions remain fragile in the south as António Guterres&nbsp;heads to Damascus for his first visit to the country as Secretary-General.

alerts.title: World News in Brief: Sexual violence in DR Congo, deaths in Ukraine and Russia, crime in Southeast Asia
alerts.summary: Independent UN human rights experts on Tuesday&nbsp;condemned the M23 armed group’s “reign of terror” over territory it controls in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), calling for an end to the Rwanda-backed group’s sexual torture and violence against civilians.

alerts.title: DR Congo: Ebola outbreak still expanding, WHO sees signs of stabilization
alerts.summary: In the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), UN health teams and partners are still racing to contain the rapidly expanding Ebola outbreak, despite ongoing insecurity in eastern areas where the virus has taken hold. As of 19 July, the DRC Government has recorded 2,423 confirmed cases, including 967 deaths and 469 recoveries.&nbsp;

alerts.title: Health response brings lifesaving care closer to thousands in Yemen
alerts.summary: Thousands of people in some of Yemen's most conflict-affected communities are receiving lifesaving health care closer to home, thanks to funding from the&nbsp;UN aid coordination office (OCHA) through the&nbsp;Yemen Humanitarian Fund.

alerts.title: Middle East: Civilians again under fire as US-Iran strikes continue
alerts.summary: As the United States and Iran trade deadly attacks for a second week, the UN continues to call for de-escalation while highlighting the devastation caused to civilians and critical infrastructure across the Middle East.

alerts.title: Ukraine: Deadly Russian attacks severely damage aid warehouses
alerts.summary: Russian attacks and drone strikes killed at least two children and injured dozens more across Ukraine over recent days while severely damaging three critical aid warehouses, UN agencies reported on Monday.

alerts.title: LIVE: Haiti endures violence and hunger, but hopes grow for a more secure future
alerts.summary: Haiti’s future success depends on “security, political engagement, and humanitarian action reinforcing each other,” the UN’s most senior official in Haiti has told the UN Security Council which met in New York on Monday to discuss the way forward for the embattled Caribbean island nation.

alerts.title: Moon Day focuses growing concerns around space junk
alerts.summary: Amid surging lunar exploration, the UN on Monday urged governments to ensure that humanity's return to the Moon does not come at the expense of planet Earth – or the long-term sustainability of space.

alerts.title: Ukraine: UN condemns overnight attack on Kyiv
alerts.summary: United Nations agencies have deplored a fresh Russian assault on the Ukrainian capital Kyiv which left at least one person dead and a dozen injured.

alerts.title: Haiti: Signs of hope despite gang violence and political uncertainty
alerts.summary: For millions of Haitians, daily life has become a struggle to stay safe, find enough food and simply move around their own neighbourhood.

alerts.title: US-Iran war: UN chief calls for new diplomatic push following attacks on civilian areas
alerts.summary: The UN Secretary-General remains deeply concerned by the continuing deadly military escalation between Iran and the United States, his spokesperson said on Friday as attacks destroyed key civilian and military infrastructure across the Gulf region.

alerts.title: World News in Brief: Appeal for calm in Kashmir, women on the move, Ebola deaths mount, 12th anniversary of MH17
alerts.summary: The&nbsp;UN human rights chief, Volker Türk, has&nbsp;appealed for calm in Pakistan-Administered Kashmir amid unrest ahead of regional elections.

alerts.title: Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid
alerts.summary: UN humanitarians working in war-torn Sudan on Friday highlighted rapidly growing needs among the more than 100,000 displaced people now sheltering in camps in the city of El Obeid.&nbsp;

alerts.title: Guterres: AI must be shaped by 'all of humanity', not a handful of powers
alerts.summary: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Friday urged governments and technology companies to work together to ensure artificial intelligence (AI) benefits all countries.

alerts.title: Peacekeepers step up support as families return to south Lebanon
alerts.summary: With violence in south Lebanon significantly decreased since mid-June, UN peacekeepers there have returned to core functions such as protecting humanitarian aid and restoring critical infrastructure as families continue to return home.

alerts.title: Gaza farmers battle to rebuild as WFP warns of West Bank settler violence
alerts.summary: The UN is supporting farmers in Gaza who are struggling to rebuild their smallholdings in areas devastated by the Hamas-Israel war.

alerts.title: Gulf crisis: Guterres calls for de-escalation, warns against return to full-on war
alerts.summary: UN Secretary-General António Guterres voiced deep concern on Wednesday over the continuing military escalation in the Middle East amid ongoing strikes by the United States and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz.&nbsp;

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: International Criminal Court briefs on Darfur
alerts.summary: On Wednesday, the Security Council heard an update on the International Criminal Court's work in Sudan's Darfur region, referred to the Court back in 2005. Focus has recently turned to atrocities in the ongoing war between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). The briefing was held as the ICC faces mounting pressure: this week, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio launched a campaign to “dismantle” the Court, which prosecutes individuals for genocide, war crimes, crimes against humanity and the crime of aggression. Follow full live coverage below.

alerts.title: Colombia: Pockets of insecurity challenge ‘vibrant democracy’, Security Council told
alerts.summary: As Colombia awaits the inauguration of its newly elected president, pockets of insecurity remain a challenge for a nation that has overcome decades of conflict, the head of the UN verification team there told the Security Council on Wednesday.

alerts.title: Looted gold and gum arabic are bankrolling Sudan's war, UN warns
alerts.summary: Alongside gold, the little-known Sudanese commodity gum arabic&nbsp;–&nbsp;used in soft drinks, food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals&nbsp;–&nbsp;continues to fuel the war.&nbsp;

alerts.title: ‘Cycle of escalation must end’: UN condemns deadly Strait of Hormuz attacks
alerts.summary: The UN maritime agency, IMO, condemned overnight attacks&nbsp;on shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz that killed at least two seafarers as fresh strikes were reported early Tuesday in the escalating US-Iran war.

alerts.title: West Africa and the Sahel: Terrorism is changing its face
alerts.summary: Jihadist groups are no longer content with launching attacks in West Africa and the Sahel: they administer territories, control trade routes, exploit new technologies and are gradually pushing their influence all the way to the Gulf of Guinea.&nbsp;

alerts.title: ‘This is a fire’: DRC Ebola outbreak is fastest-growing ever, warns WHO
alerts.summary: Infections of the Bundibugyo species of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have reached record highs and a majority of new cases are coming from “unknown chains of transmission”, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Tuesday.

alerts.title: Strait of Hormuz: UN agencies call for diplomacy to end attacks
alerts.summary: As both Washington and Tehran claim to control the critical commercial shipping route through the narrow Strait of Hormuz, UN agencies on Monday are calling for de-escalation amid the recent spike in strikes in the region related to the US-Iran war.

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: Top UN officials call for de-escalation in Yemen
alerts.summary: As the UN chief’s Special Envoy for Yemen, Hans Grundberg, calls for swift de-escalation following reports of Saudi Arabian airstrikes and Iranian aircraft landing in the country, the Security Council’s emergency meeting at 3pm (local time) on Monday heard briefings from top officials on the latest humanitarian and political situation.

alerts.title: UN chief urges Iran and US to ‘urgently resume negotiations’ as Gulf strikes escalate
alerts.summary: Renewed strikes and counterstrikes between Iran and the United States in the Gulf region have raised fears of a return to all‑out war, with Washington denying Tehran’s claim that it had closed the crucial Strait of Hormuz on Sunday.

alerts.title: Reviving communities home by home in Gaza
alerts.summary: Displacement has drastically reshaped every aspect of daily life in war-ravaged Gaza for 24-year-old Mayyada, who is seven months pregnant with twins and caring for her two-year-old daughter, but now she and her family are getting settled into a temporary home of their own.

alerts.title: ‘We face a new ordeal’: Gaza's search for the dead goes on
alerts.summary: After thousands of buildings were destroyed in the Gaza Strip during the Israel-Hamas war, local teams and civil defense personnel continue the arduous and delicate mission to remove rubble and search for the remains of missing persons believed to still be buried under the debris of homes destroyed by Israeli airstrikes.

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: ‘Lost continuity of knowledge’ on Iran’s nuclear programme since US-Israel attacks, top UN official warns
alerts.summary: Since the United States and Israel launched attacks on Iran in late February, there has been a “lost continuity of knowledge” on Tehran’s nuclear programme, Rosemary DiCarlo, the UN peace affairs chief, warned the Security Council on Friday as tensions mounted amid new strikes across the Middle East.

alerts.title: Diplomats go virtual to witness Gaza displacement site up close
alerts.summary: Representatives from 12 countries carried out a “virtual diplomatic field visit” to a displacement site in the Gaza Strip and heard from some of the residents about their pressing needs, the United Nations said on Thursday.&nbsp;

alerts.title: US-Iran war leaves shipping at near-standstill in Hormuz again
alerts.summary: Renewed hostilities between the United States and Iran reportedly brought shipping to a near-standstill in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, leaving around 6,000 seafarers stranded aboard hundreds of vessels and Gulf countries on high alert for further attacks.

alerts.title: UN Syria inquiry presses for answers on missing detainees after wide-ranging visit
alerts.summary: UN investigators have urged Syria’s Government to trace thousands of missing detainees and hold perpetrators to account after a week-long visit that took in bomb attacks in Damascus, prison visits in the northeast and reports of vigilante violence in Homs.

alerts.title: US-Iran war: Renewed attacks in Strait of Hormuz prompt another global energy alert
alerts.summary: Renewed attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz unsettled energy markets on Wednesday and prompted calls from the UN maritime agency, IMO, for “maximum restraint and de-escalation”.

alerts.title: Grim homecoming: Devastation greets Lebanon’s war-weary returnees
alerts.summary: The ceasefire deal between Beirut and Tel Aviv has led to a marked reduction in hostilities between Hezbollah and the Israeli military, sparked by the wider Iran-Israel-US war, but the situation remains uncertain for communities returning home only to find widespread destruction, UN peacekeepers said on Wednesday.

alerts.title: Occupied Palestinian Territory: Aid restrictions in Gaza, ‘senseless’ infant deaths in the West Bank
alerts.summary: Ongoing restrictions and closures of border crossings continue to hamper delivery of critical supplies into the Gaza Strip, amid mounting concern for children there and in the West Bank, the United Nations said on Monday.&nbsp;

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Iranian attack in Bahrain
alerts.summary: The 15-member Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Thursday morning to address the escalating tensions in the Gulf region following Iran attacks in recent days on Bahrain and Kuwait while Tehran and Washington continue to trade strikes over the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint. Stay tuned for live updates.

alerts.title: Expanding areas under Israeli control in Gaza increase risks to civilians, UN warns
alerts.summary: The continued expansion of areas under Israeli control in Gaza since October’s ceasefire agreement is placing civilians at greater risk and severely constraining humanitarian operations, the UN and humanitarian partners&nbsp;warned on Wednesday.

alerts.title: Despite record $100 million shortfall, Palestine relief agency still ‘a critical platform’ for Gaza recovery
alerts.summary: The UN agency serving 5.9 million Palestine refugees, UNRWA, continues to strive to deliver on its mandate while facing an unprecedented $100 million budget shortfall, a gap it hopes to narrow during Tuesday’s pledging conference at UN Headquarters.

alerts.title: Strait of Hormuz: Gradual re-opening is no quick fix for developing nations, UN warns
alerts.summary: Re-opening the Strait of Hormuz would bring vital relief for many economies, but developing countries will continue to grapple with increased food and fuel costs, according to&nbsp;a new UN report released on Tuesday.
